To address this, we introduce the IRIS Benchmark, to our knowledge the first benchmark designed to synchronously evaluate the fairness of both understanding and generation tasks in UMLLMs. Enabled by our demographic classifier, ARES, and four supporting large-scale datasets, the benchmark is designed to normalize and aggregate arbitrary metrics into a high-dimensional "fairness space'', integrating 60 granular metrics across three dimensions-Ideal Fairness, Real-world Fidelity, and Bias Inertia & Steerability (IRIS). Through this benchmark, our evaluation of leading UMLLMs uncovers systemic phenomena such as the "generation gap'', individual inconsistencies like "personality splits'', and the "counter-stereotype reward'', while offering diagnostics to guide the optimization of their fairness capabilities. With its novel and extensible framework, the IRIS benchmark is capable of integrating evolving fairness metrics, ultimately helping to resolve the "Tower of Babel'' impasse. Project Page: https://iris-benchmark-web.vercel.app/

zh.title: 公平的想法，公平的行动？UMLLMs中理解和生成的同步基准
zh.summary: IRIS基准通过60个细粒度指标全面评估多模态大语言模型在理解和生成任务中的公平性。它识别了系统性的公平性问题，并提出了诊断改进方法。

fr.title: Justice d'esprit, justice d'action ? Un benchmark synchrone pour la compréhension et la génération dans les UMLLMs
fr.summary: Le benchmark IRIS évalue de manière complète l'équité dans les LLM multimodaux à travers les tâches de compréhension et de génération en utilisant 60 métriques granulaires. Il identifie les problèmes d'équité systémique et propose des approches diagnostiques pour l'amélioration.

de.title: Fair in Gedanke, fair in Aktion? Ein synchrones Benchmark für Verständnis und Generierung in UMLLMs
de.summary: Die IRIS-Benchmark bewertet umfassend die Fairness in multimodalen LLM über Verständnis- und Generierungsaufgaben mit 60 granularen Metriken. Sie identifiziert systemische Fairness-Probleme und schlägt diagnostische Ansätze zur Verbesserung vor.

es.title: ¿Justo en mente, justo en acción? Un benchmark sincrónico para la comprensión y generación en UMLLMs
es.summary: El benchmark IRIS evalúa integralmente la equidad en los LLM multimodales en tareas de comprensión y generación utilizando 60 métricas granulares. Identifica problemas de equidad sistémica y propone enfoques diagnósticos para la mejora.

title: Heterophily-Agnostic Hypergraph Neural Networks with Riemannian Local Exchanger
summary: HealHGNN is a neural network architecture for hypergraphs that handles both homophilic and heterophilic graphs using Riemannian geometry. It achieves state-of-the-art performance while maintaining linear complexity.
content:
arXiv:2603.00599v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Hypergraphs are the natural description of higher-order interactions among objects, widely applied in social network analysis, cross-modal retrieval, etc. Hypergraph Neural Networks (HGNNs) have become the dominant solution for learning on hypergraphs. Traditional HGNNs are extended from message passing graph neural networks, following the homophily assumption, and thus struggle with the prevalent heterophilic hypergraphs that call for long-range dependence modeling. In this paper, we achieve heterophily-agnostic message passing through the lens of Riemannian geometry. The key insight lies in the connection between oversquashing and hypergraph bottleneck within the framework of Riemannian manifold heat flow. Building on this, we propose the novel idea of locally adapting the bottlenecks of different subhypergraphs. The core innovation of the proposed mechanism is the design of an adaptive local (heat) exchanger. Specifically, it captures the rich long-range dependencies via the Robin condition, and preserves the representation distinguishability via source terms, thereby enabling heterophily-agnostic message passing with theoretical guarantees. Based on this theoretical foundation, we present a novel Heat-Exchanger with Adaptive Locality for Hypergraph Neural Network (HealHGNN), designed as a node-hyperedge bidirectional systems with linear complexity in the number of nodes and hyperedges. Extensive experiments on both homophilic and heterophilic cases show that HealHGNN achieves the state-of-the-art performance.

zh.title: 异质性无关超图神经网络与黎曼局部交换器
zh.summary: HealHGNN是一个超图神经网络架构，使用黎曼几何处理同质和异质图。它实现了最先进的性能，同时保持线性复杂度。

fr.title: Réseaux de Neurones Hypergraphiques Agnostiques à l'Hétérophilie avec Échangeur Local Riemannien
fr.summary: HealHGNN est une architecture de réseau de neurones pour les hypergraphes qui traite les graphes homophiles et hétérophiles en utilisant la géométrie riemannienne. Elle réalise des performances de pointe tout en maintenant une complexité linéaire.

de.title: Heterophilie-agnostische Hypergraph-Neuronale Netzwerke mit Riemannscher lokaler Austauscheinheit
de.summary: HealHGNN ist eine neuronale Netzwerk-Architektur für Hypergraphen, die sowohl homophile als auch heterophile Graphen unter Verwendung der Riemannschen Geometrie verarbeitet. Es erreicht modernste Leistung bei Beibehaltung linearer Komplexität.

es.title: Redes Neuronales de Hipergrafos Agnósticas a la Heterofilia con Intercambiador Local Riemanniano
es.summary: HealHGNN es una arquitectura de red neuronal para hipergrafos que maneja gráficos homofílicos y heterofílicos utilizando geometría de Riemann. Logra un rendimiento de última generación mientras mantiene complejidad lineal.

title: Machine Learning Grade Prediction Using Students' Grades and Demographics
summary: Researchers develop a unified machine learning framework that simultaneously predicts student pass/fail outcomes and continuous grades using academic and demographic data. Classification models achieved 96% accuracy, enabling early identification of at-risk students.
content:
arXiv:2603.00608v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Student repetition in secondary education imposes significant resource burdens, particularly in resource-constrained contexts. Addressing this challenge, this study introduces a unified machine learning framework that simultaneously predicts pass/fail outcomes and continuous grades, a departure from prior research that treats classification and regression as separate tasks. Six models were evaluated: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, and Random Forest for classification, and Linear Regression, Decision Tree Regressor, and Random Forest Regressor for regression, with hyperparameters optimized via exhaustive grid search. Using academic and demographic data from 4424 secondary school students, classification models achieved accuracies of up to 96%, while regression models attained a coefficient of determination of 0.70, surpassing baseline approaches. These results confirm the feasibility of early, data-driven identification of at-risk students and highlight the value of integrating dual-task prediction for more comprehensive insights. By enabling timely, personalized interventions, the framework offers a practical pathway to reducing grade repetition and optimizing resource allocation.

zh.title: 使用学生成绩和人口统计数据的机器学习成绩预测
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一个统一的机器学习框架，使用学术和人口统计数据同时预测学生及格/不及格的结果和连续成绩。分类模型达到了96%的准确率，能够早期识别风险学生。

fr.title: Prédiction des Notes avec Apprentissage Automatique Utilisant les Notes et Données Démographiques des Étudiants
fr.summary: Les chercheurs développent un framework d'apprentissage automatique unifié qui prédit simultanément les résultats de réussite/échec et les notes continues des étudiants en utilisant les données académiques et démographiques. Les modèles de classification ont atteint une précision de 96%, permettant l'identification précoce des étudiants à risque.

de.title: Maschinelles Lernen zur Notenvorhersage mit Schülernoten und demografischen Daten
de.summary: Forscher entwickeln ein einheitliches Framework für maschinelles Lernen, das gleichzeitig Bestehen/Durchfallen-Ergebnisse und kontinuierliche Noten der Schüler unter Verwendung akademischer und demografischer Daten vorhersagt. Klassifizierungsmodelle erreichten eine Genauigkeit von 96% und ermöglichen die frühzeitige Identifizierung gefährdeter Schüler.

es.title: Predicción de Calificaciones con Aprendizaje Automático Utilizando Calificaciones y Datos Demográficos de Estudiantes
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollan un marco unificado de aprendizaje automático que predice simultáneamente los resultados de aprobado/reprobado y las calificaciones continuas de los estudiantes utilizando datos académicos y demográficos. Los modelos de clasificación lograron una precisión del 96%, permitiendo la identificación temprana de estudiantes en riesgo.

title: TraceSIR: A Multi-Agent Framework for Structured Analysis and Reporting of Agentic Execution Traces
summary: TraceSIR provides tools for analyzing complex LLM agent execution traces by compressing them while preserving behavioral information and performing fine-grained diagnosis. It enables better debugging and understanding of agent behavior at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.00623v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic systems augment large language models with external tools and iterative decision making, enabling complex tasks such as deep research, function calling, and coding. However, their long and intricate execution traces make failure diagnosis and root cause analysis extremely challenging. Manual inspection does not scale, while directly applying LLMs to raw traces is hindered by input length limits and unreliable reasoning. Focusing solely on final task outcomes further discards critical behavioral information required for accurate issue localization. To address these issues, we propose TraceSIR, a multi-agent framework for structured analysis and reporting of agentic execution traces. TraceSIR coordinates three specialized agents: (1) StructureAgent, which introduces a novel abstraction format, TraceFormat, to compress execution traces while preserving essential behavioral information; (2) InsightAgent, which performs fine-grained diagnosis including issue localization, root cause analysis, and optimization suggestions; (3) ReportAgent, which aggregates insights across task instances and generates comprehensive analysis reports. To evaluate TraceSIR, we construct TraceBench, covering three real-world agentic scenarios, and introduce ReportEval, an evaluation protocol for assessing the quality and usability of analysis reports aligned with industry needs. Experiments show that TraceSIR consistently produces coherent, informative, and actionable reports, significantly outperforming existing approaches across all evaluation dimensions. Our project and video are publicly available at https://github.com/SHU-XUN/TraceSIR.

zh.title: TraceSIR：代理执行跟踪的结构化分析和报告多代理框架
zh.summary: TraceSIR提供了分析复杂LLM代理执行跟踪的工具，通过压缩同时保留行为信息并进行细粒度诊断。它能够更好地调试和理解大规模的代理行为。

fr.title: TraceSIR : Un Framework Multi-Agent pour l'Analyse Structurée et la Génération de Rapports sur les Traces d'Exécution des Agents
fr.summary: TraceSIR fournit des outils pour analyser les traces d'exécution complexes des agents LLM en les comprimant tout en préservant les informations comportementales et en effectuant un diagnostic fin. Il permet un meilleur débogage et une meilleure compréhension du comportement des agents à grande échelle.

de.title: TraceSIR: Ein Multi-Agent-Framework für strukturierte Analyse und Berichterstattung von Agenten-Ausführungstraces
de.summary: TraceSIR bietet Tools zur Analyse komplexer LLM-Agent-Ausführungstraces, indem diese komprimiert werden und dabei Verhaltenssinformationen bewahrt sowie feinkörnige Diagnosen durchgeführt werden. Es ermöglicht besseres Debugging und besseres Verständnis des Agent-Verhaltens im großen Maßstab.

es.title: TraceSIR: Un Marco Multi-Agente para Análisis Estructurado e Informes de Trazas de Ejecución de Agentes
es.summary: TraceSIR proporciona herramientas para analizar trazas de ejecución complejas de agentes LLM comprimiéndolas mientras se preserva la información de comportamiento y se realiza diagnóstico de grano fino. Permite un mejor depuración y comprensión del comportamiento del agente a escala.

title: LiTS: A Modular Framework for LLM Tree Search
summary: LiTS is a modular Python framework that simplifies implementing tree search algorithms like MCTS for LLM reasoning tasks. It demonstrates that policy diversity, not reward quality, is the key bottleneck for effective tree search.
content:
arXiv:2603.00631v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LiTS is a modular Python framework for LLM reasoning via tree search. It decomposes tree search into three reusable components (Policy, Transition, and RewardModel) that plug into algorithms like MCTS and BFS. A decorator-based registry enables domain experts to extend to new domains by registering components, and algorithmic researchers to implement custom search algorithms. We demonstrate composability on MATH500 (language reasoning), Crosswords (environment planning), and MapEval (tool use), showing that components and algorithms are orthogonal: components are reusable across algorithms within each task type, and algorithms work across all components and domains. We also report a mode-collapse finding: in infinite action spaces, LLM policy diversity (not reward quality) is the bottleneck for effective tree search. A demonstration video is available at https://youtu.be/nRGX43YrR3I. The package is released under the Apache 2.0 license at https://github.com/xinzhel/lits-llm, including installation instructions and runnable examples that enable users to reproduce the demonstrated workflows.

zh.title: LiTS：大语言模型树搜索的模块化框架
zh.summary: LiTS是一个模块化的Python框架，可以简化为LLM推理任务实现树搜索算法（如MCTS）。它证明了策略多样性而非奖励质量是有效树搜索的关键瓶颈。

fr.title: LiTS : Un Framework Modulaire pour la Recherche d'Arbres LLM
fr.summary: LiTS est un framework Python modulaire qui simplifie l'implémentation des algorithmes de recherche d'arbres comme MCTS pour les tâches de raisonnement LLM. Il démontre que la diversité des stratégies, et non la qualité des récompenses, est le goulot d'étranglement clé pour une recherche d'arbres efficace.

de.title: LiTS: Ein modulares Framework für die Baumsuche von LLM
de.summary: LiTS ist ein modulares Python-Framework, das die Implementierung von Baumsuchalgorithmen wie MCTS für LLM-Aufgaben vereinfacht. Es zeigt, dass Strategievielfalt, nicht Belohnungsqualität, der Schlüsselbottleneck für effektive Baumsuche ist.

es.title: LiTS: Un Framework Modular para la Búsqueda de Árboles LLM
es.summary: LiTS es un framework Python modular que simplifica la implementación de algoritmos de búsqueda de árboles como MCTS para tareas de razonamiento de LLM. Demuestra que la diversidad de políticas, no la calidad de las recompensas, es el cuello de botella clave para una búsqueda de árboles efectiva.

title: InfoPO: Information-Driven Policy Optimization for User-Centric Agents
summary: InfoPO is a reinforcement learning method that optimizes multi-turn agent-user interactions by rewarding turns that reduce uncertainty and improve decisions. It outperforms baselines on intent clarification, coding, and decision-making tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.00656v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world user requests to LLM agents are often underspecified. Agents must interact to acquire missing information and make correct downstream decisions. However, current multi-turn GRPO-based methods often rely on trajectory-level reward computation, which leads to credit assignment problems and insufficient advantage signals within rollout groups. A feasible approach is to identify valuable interaction turns at a fine granularity to drive more targeted learning. To address this, we introduce InfoPO (Information-Driven Policy Optimization), which frames multi-turn interaction as a process of active uncertainty reduction and computes an information-gain reward that credits turns whose feedback measurably changes the agent's subsequent action distribution compared to a masked-feedback counterfactual. It then combines this signal with task outcomes via an adaptive variance-gated fusion to identify information importance while maintaining task-oriented goal direction. Across diverse tasks, including intent clarification, collaborative coding, and tool-augmented decision making, InfoPO consistently outperforms prompting and multi-turn RL baselines. It also demonstrates robustness under user simulator shifts and generalizes effectively to environment-interactive tasks. Overall, InfoPO provides a principled and scalable mechanism for optimizing complex agent-user collaboration. Code is available at https://github.com/kfq20/InfoPO.

zh.title: InfoPO：用户中心代理的信息驱动策略优化
zh.summary: InfoPO是一种强化学习方法，通过奖励能减少不确定性并改进决策的回合来优化多轮代理-用户交互。它在意图澄清、编码和决策任务上超越了基线性能。

fr.title: InfoPO : Optimisation de Politique Guidée par l'Information pour les Agents Centrés sur l'Utilisateur
fr.summary: InfoPO est une méthode d'apprentissage par renforcement qui optimise les interactions multi-tours entre agent et utilisateur en récompensant les tours qui réduisent l'incertitude et améliorent les décisions. Elle surpasse les modèles de base dans les tâches de clarification d'intention, de codage et de prise de décision.

de.title: InfoPO: Informationsgesteuerte Richtlinienoptimierung für benutzerorientierte Agenten
de.summary: InfoPO ist eine Reinforcement-Learning-Methode, die Mehrweg-Agent-Benutzer-Interaktionen optimiert, indem sie Wendungen belohnt, die Unsicherheit reduzieren und Entscheidungen verbessern. Sie übertrifft Baselines bei Aufgaben zur Absichtserklärung, Codierung und Entscheidungsfindung.

es.title: InfoPO: Optimización de Políticas Impulsada por Información para Agentes Centrados en el Usuario
es.summary: InfoPO es un método de aprendizaje por refuerzo que optimiza las interacciones multiturno entre agente y usuario recompensando turnos que reducen la incertidumbre y mejoran las decisiones. Supera los baselines en tareas de aclaración de intenciones, codificación y toma de decisiones.

title: K^2-Agent: Co-Evolving Know-What and Know-How for Hierarchical Mobile Device Control
summary: K2-Agent is a hierarchical agent for mobile device control that separates and co-evolves task knowledge and execution skills through self-reflection and curriculum learning. It achieves 76.1% success on complex Android tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.00676v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Existing mobile device control agents often perform poorly when solving complex tasks requiring long-horizon planning and precise operations, typically due to a lack of relevant task experience or unfamiliarity with skill execution. We propose K2-Agent, a hierarchical framework that models human-like cognition by separating and co-evolving declarative (knowing what) and procedural (knowing how) knowledge for planning and execution. K2-Agent's high level reasoner is bootstrapped from a single demonstration per task and runs a Summarize-Reflect-Locate-Revise (SRLR) loop to distill and iteratively refine task-level declarative knowledge through self-evolution. The low-level executor is trained with our curriculum-guided Group Relative Policy Optimization (C-GRPO), which (i) constructs a balanced sample pool using decoupled reward signals and (ii) employs dynamic demonstration injection to guide the model in autonomously generating successful trajectories for training. On the challenging AndroidWorld benchmark, K2-Agent achieves a 76.1% success rate using only raw screenshots and open-source backbones. Furthermore, K2-Agent shows powerful dual generalization: its high-level declarative knowledge transfers across diverse base models, while its low-level procedural skills achieve competitive performance on unseen tasks in ScreenSpot-v2 and Android-in-the-Wild (AitW).

zh.title: K²-Agent：分层移动设备控制的知识和能力协同演化
zh.summary: K2-Agent是一个用于移动设备控制的分层代理，通过自反思和课程学习来分离和协同演化任务知识和执行技能。它在复杂的Android任务上达到了76.1%的成功率。

fr.title: K²-Agent : Co-évolution du Savoir-Quoi et du Savoir-Faire pour le Contrôle Hiérarchique des Appareils Mobiles
fr.summary: K2-Agent est un agent hiérarchique pour le contrôle des appareils mobiles qui sépare et co-évolue les connaissances de tâche et les compétences d'exécution par l'auto-réflexion et l'apprentissage par curriculum. Il atteint 76,1% de succès sur les tâches Android complexes.

de.title: K²-Agent: Co-Evolving Know-What and Know-How für hierarchische Steuerung mobiler Geräte
de.summary: K2-Agent ist ein hierarchischer Agent zur Steuerung mobiler Geräte, der Aufgabenwissen und Ausführungsfähigkeiten durch Selbstreflexion und Lernwechsel trennt und koevolviert. Er erreicht 76,1% Erfolgsquote bei komplexen Android-Aufgaben.

es.title: K²-Agent: Coevolución de Saber-Qué y Saber-Cómo para Control Jerárquico de Dispositivos Móviles
es.summary: K2-Agent es un agente jerárquico para el control de dispositivos móviles que separa y coevoluciona el conocimiento de tareas y habilidades de ejecución a través de autorreflexión y aprendizaje por currículum. Logra una tasa de éxito del 76,1% en tareas complejas de Android.

title: MemPO: Self-Memory Policy Optimization for Long-Horizon Agents
summary: MemPO introduces a novel algorithm enabling AI agents to autonomously manage and summarize their memory during extended tasks, reducing token consumption by up to 73% while improving performance. This advancement addresses the context bottleneck that limits long-horizon AI agent capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.00680v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long-horizon agents face the challenge of growing context size during interaction with environment, which degrades the performance and stability. Existing methods typically introduce the external memory module and look up the relevant information from the stored memory, which prevents the model itself from proactively managing its memory content and aligning with the agent's overarching task objectives. To address these limitations, we propose the self-memory policy optimization algorithm (MemPO), which enables the agent (policy model) to autonomously summarize and manage their memory during interaction with environment. By improving the credit assignment mechanism based on memory effectiveness, the policy model can selectively retain crucial information, significantly reducing token consumption while preserving task performance. Extensive experiments and analyses confirm that MemPO achieves absolute F1 score gains of 25.98% over the base model and 7.1% over the previous SOTA baseline, while reducing token usage by 67.58% and 73.12%.

zh.title: MemPO：长视界智能体的自记忆策略优化
zh.summary: MemPO引入了一种新颖的算法，使人工智能代理能够在长期任务中自主管理和总结其记忆，将令牌消耗减少多达73%，同时提高性能。这一进步解决了限制长视界人工智能代理能力的上下文瓶颈。

fr.title: MemPO : Optimisation de politique auto-mémoire pour les agents à long horizon
fr.summary: MemPO introduit un nouvel algorithme permettant aux agents IA de gérer et de résumer de façon autonome leur mémoire lors de tâches prolongées, réduisant la consommation de jetons jusqu'à 73% tout en améliorant les performances. Cette avancée résout le goulot d'étranglement contextuel qui limite les capacités des agents IA à long horizon.

de.title: MemPO: Self-Memory Policy Optimization für langfristige Agenten
de.summary: MemPO führt einen neuartigen Algorithmus ein, der KI-Agenten ermöglicht, ihre Erinnerungen während erweiterter Aufgaben autonom zu verwalten und zusammenzufassen, die Tokenverbrauch um bis zu 73% reduziert und gleichzeitig die Leistung verbessert. Diese Weiterentwicklung behebt den Kontextengpass, der die Fähigkeiten langfristiger KI-Agenten einschränkt.

es.title: MemPO: Optimización de Política de Auto-Memoria para Agentes de Largo Horizonte
es.summary: MemPO introduce un algoritmo novedoso que permite a los agentes de IA gestionar y resumir autónomamente su memoria durante tareas prolongadas, reduciendo el consumo de tokens hasta en un 73% mientras mejora el rendimiento. Este avance aborda el cuello de botella contextual que limita las capacidades del agente de IA de largo horizonte.

title: AIoT-based Continuous, Contextualized, and Explainable Driving Assessment for Older Adults
summary: AURA is an AI-powered framework that continuously monitors driving safety for older adults using in-vehicle sensing and machine learning. The system distinguishes age-related performance changes from situational factors, enabling personalized interventions to support safer driving.
content:
arXiv:2603.00691v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The world is undergoing a major demographic shift as older adults become a rapidly growing share of the population, creating new challenges for driving safety. In car-dependent regions such as the United States, driving remains essential for independence, access to services, and social participation. At the same time, aging can introduce gradual changes in vision, attention, reaction time, and driving control that quietly reduce safety. Today's assessment methods rely largely on infrequent clinic visits or simple screening tools, offering only a brief snapshot and failing to reflect how an older adult actually drives on the road. Our work starts from the observation that everyday driving provides a continuous record of functional ability and captures how a driver responds to traffic, navigates complex roads, and manages routine behavior. Leveraging this insight, we propose AURA, an Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) framework for continuous, real-world assessment of driving safety among older adults. AURA integrates richer in-vehicle sensing, multi-scale behavioral modeling, and context-aware analysis to extract detailed indicators of driving performance from routine trips. It organizes fine-grained actions into longer behavioral trajectories and separates age-related performance changes from situational factors such as traffic, road design, or weather. By integrating sensing, modeling, and interpretation within a privacy-preserving edge architecture, AURA provides a foundation for proactive, individualized support that helps older adults drive safely. This paper outlines the design principles, challenges, and research opportunities needed to build reliable, real-world monitoring systems that promote safer aging behind the wheel.

zh.title: 基于物联网人工智能的老年人连续、情境化、可解释的驾驶评估
zh.summary: AURA是一个由人工智能驱动的框架，使用车载传感和机器学习持续监测老年人的驾驶安全。该系统将与年龄相关的性能变化与情境因素区分开来，能够进行个性化干预以支持更安全的驾驶。

fr.title: Évaluation continue, contextualisée et explicable de la conduite pour les personnes âgées basée sur l'AIoT
fr.summary: AURA est un cadre alimenté par l'IA qui surveille en continu la sécurité de la conduite pour les personnes âgées en utilisant des capteurs embarqués et l'apprentissage automatique. Le système distingue les changements de performance liés à l'âge des facteurs situationnels, permettant des interventions personnalisées pour soutenir une conduite plus sûre.

de.title: AIoT-basierte kontinuierliche, kontextualisierte und erklärbare Fahrbewertung für ältere Erwachsene
de.summary: AURA ist ein KI-gestütztes Framework, das die Fahrsicherheit älterer Erwachsener kontinuierlich überwacht, indem Fahrzeugsensoren und maschinelles Lernen verwendet werden. Das System unterscheidet altersbezogene Leistungsänderungen von situativen Faktoren und ermöglicht personalisierte Interventionen zur Unterstützung sicherer Fahrten.

es.title: Evaluación Continua, Contextualizada y Explicable de Conducción para Adultos Mayores Basada en AIoT
es.summary: AURA es un marco impulsado por IA que supervisa continuamente la seguridad de conducción para adultos mayores utilizando sensores integrados en el vehículo y aprendizaje automático. El sistema distingue los cambios de desempeño relacionados con la edad de los factores situacionales, permitiendo intervenciones personalizadas para apoyar una conducción más segura.

title: MO-MIX: Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Cooperative Decision-Making With Deep Reinforcement Learning
summary: MO-MIX addresses multi-agent systems with conflicting objectives through deep reinforcement learning using centralized training and decentralized execution. The approach generates Pareto-optimal solutions while reducing computational costs compared to baseline methods.
content:
arXiv:2603.00730v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (RL) has been applied extensively to solve complex decision-making problems. In many real-world scenarios, tasks often have several conflicting objectives and may require multiple agents to cooperate, which are the multi-objective multi-agent decision-making problems. However, only few works have been conducted on this intersection. Existing approaches are limited to separate fields and can only handle multi-agent decision-making with a single objective, or multi-objective decision-making with a single agent. In this paper, we propose MO-MIX to solve the multi-objective multi-agent reinforcement learning (MOMARL) problem. Our approach is based on the centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE) framework. A weight vector representing preference over the objectives is fed into the decentralized agent network as a condition for local action-value function estimation, while a mixing network with parallel architecture is used to estimate the joint action-value function. In addition, an exploration guide approach is applied to improve the uniformity of the final non-dominated solutions. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively solve the multi-objective multi-agent cooperative decision-making problem and generate an approximation of the Pareto set. Our approach not only significantly outperforms the baseline method in all four kinds of evaluation metrics, but also requires less computational cost.

zh.title: MO-MIX：深度强化学习的多目标多智能体协作决策
zh.summary: MO-MIX通过深度强化学习解决具有冲突目标的多智能体系统问题，采用集中训练和分散执行的方式。该方法生成帕累托最优解，同时相比基线方法降低计算成本。

fr.title: MO-MIX : Prise de Décision Coopérative Multi-Agent Multi-Objectif avec Apprentissage par Renforcement Profond
fr.summary: MO-MIX aborde les systèmes multi-agents avec des objectifs conflictuels grâce à l'apprentissage par renforcement profond utilisant l'entraînement centralisé et l'exécution décentralisée. L'approche génère des solutions optimales de Pareto tout en réduisant les coûts de calcul par rapport aux méthodes de base.

de.title: MO-MIX: Multi-Ziel-Multi-Agent-Kooperative Entscheidungsfindung mit Deep Reinforcement Learning
de.summary: MO-MIX behandelt Multi-Agent-Systeme mit widersprechenden Zielen durch Deep Reinforcement Learning mit zentralisiertem Training und dezentralisierter Ausführung. Der Ansatz erzeugt Pareto-optimale Lösungen und reduziert die Rechenkosten im Vergleich zu Baseline-Methoden.

es.title: MO-MIX: Toma de Decisiones Cooperativa Multi-Agente Multi-Objetivo con Aprendizaje por Refuerzo Profundo
es.summary: MO-MIX aborda sistemas multi-agente con objetivos conflictivos mediante aprendizaje por refuerzo profundo utilizando entrenamiento centralizado y ejecución descentralizada. El enfoque genera soluciones óptimas de Pareto mientras reduce los costos computacionales en comparación con métodos de referencia.

title: The Synthetic Web: Adversarially-Curated Mini-Internets for Diagnosing Epistemic Weaknesses of Language Agents
summary: The Synthetic Web Benchmark evaluates how language agents handle adversarial misinformation in search results, revealing critical failures in current frontier models. The research demonstrates that high-plausibility false information significantly degrades agent accuracy despite access to truthful sources.
content:
arXiv:2603.00801v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Language agents increasingly act as web-enabled systems that search, browse, and synthesize information from diverse sources. However, these sources can include unreliable or adversarial content, and the robustness of agents to adversarial ranking - where misleading information appears prominently in search results - remains poorly understood. Existing benchmarks evaluate functional navigation or static factuality but cannot causally isolate this vulnerability, and current mitigation strategies for retrieval-augmented generation remain largely untested under such conditions. We introduce Synthetic Web Benchmark, a procedurally generated environment comprising thousands of hyperlinked articles with ground-truth labels for credibility and factuality, process-level interaction traces, and contamination filtering to eliminate training-data leakage. By injecting a single high-plausibility misinformation article into a controllable search rank, we measure the causal effect of adversarial exposure in six frontier models. The results reveal catastrophic failures: accuracy collapses despite unlimited access to truthful sources, with minimal search escalation and severe miscalibration. These findings expose fundamental limitations in how current frontier models handle conflicting information, with immediate implications for deployment in high-stakes domains. Our benchmark enables systematic analysis of these failure modes and provides a controlled testbed for evaluating mitigation strategies under adversarial ranking - a gap in current research. This work establishes a reproducible baseline for developing search-robust and epistemically humble agents capable of resisting manipulation in high-stakes domains.

zh.title: 合成网络：用于诊断语言智能体认知弱点的对抗策展迷你互联网
zh.summary: 合成网络基准评估语言智能体如何处理搜索结果中的对抗性错误信息，揭示了当前前沿模型的关键失败。研究表明，高可信度的虚假信息尽管可以访问真实信息来源，但仍会显著降低智能体的准确性。

fr.title: Le Web Synthétique : Mini-Internets Curatés Adversariellement pour Diagnostiquer les Faiblesses Épistémiques des Agents Linguistiques
fr.summary: L'indice de référence du Web Synthétique évalue comment les agents linguistiques traitent la désinformation adversariale dans les résultats de recherche, révélant des défaillances critiques dans les modèles de pointe actuels. La recherche démontre que l'information fausse hautement plausible dégrade significativement la précision de l'agent malgré l'accès à des sources véridiques.

de.title: Das Synthetische Web: Adversarisch Kuratierte Mini-Internets zur Diagnose Epistemischer Schwächen von Sprachagenten
de.summary: Der Synthetic Web Benchmark bewertet, wie Sprachagenten mit adversarieller Fehlinformation in Suchergebnissen umgehen und enthüllt kritische Fehler in aktuellen Frontier-Modellen. Die Forschung zeigt, dass hochplausible falsche Informationen die Genauigkeit des Agenten trotz Zugang zu wahren Quellen erheblich verschlechtert.

es.title: La Web Sintética: Mini-Internets Curados Adversarialmente para Diagnosticar Debilidades Epistémicas de Agentes Lingüísticos
es.summary: El Benchmark de la Web Sintética evalúa cómo los agentes lingüísticos manejan la desinformación adversarial en los resultados de búsqueda, revelando fallos críticos en los modelos de vanguardia actuales. La investigación demuestra que la información falsa de alta plausibilidad degrada significativamente la precisión del agente a pesar del acceso a fuentes veraces.

title: MetaMind: General and Cognitive World Models in Multi-Agent Systems by Meta-Theory of Mind
summary: MetaMind introduces a meta-theory of mind framework that enables AI agents to predict other agents' goals and beliefs from observed behavior without explicit communication. This self-reflective approach allows agents to adaptively coordinate complex multi-agent tasks with superior performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.00808v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: A major challenge for world models in multi-agent systems is to understand interdependent agent dynamics, predict interactive multi-agent trajectories, and plan over long horizons with collective awareness, without centralized supervision or explicit communication. In this paper, MetaMind, a general and cognitive world model for multi-agent systems that leverages a novel meta-theory of mind (Meta-ToM) framework, is proposed. Through MetaMind, each agent learns not only to predict and plan over its own beliefs, but also to inversely reason goals and beliefs from its own behavior trajectories. This self-reflective, bidirectional inference loop enables each agent to learn a metacognitive ability in a self-supervised manner. Then, MetaMind is shown to generalize the metacognitive ability from first-person to third-person through analogical reasoning. Thus, in multi-agent systems, each agent with MetaMind can actively reason about goals and beliefs of other agents from limited, observable behavior trajectories in a zero-shot manner, and then adapt to emergent collective intention without an explicit communication mechanism. Extended simulation results on diverse multi-agent tasks demonstrate that MetaMind can achieve superior task performance and outperform baselines in few-shot multi-agent generalization.

zh.title: MetaMind：通过心智元理论的多智能体系统中的通用认知世界模型
zh.summary: MetaMind引入了心智元理论框架，使AI智能体能够从观察行为中预测其他智能体的目标和信念，无需明确通信。这种自我反思的方法允许智能体自适应地协调复杂的多智能体任务，性能优越。

fr.title: MetaMind : Modèles du Monde Généraux et Cognitifs dans les Systèmes Multi-Agents par Méta-Théorie de l'Esprit
fr.summary: MetaMind introduit un cadre de méta-théorie de l'esprit qui permet aux agents IA de prédire les objectifs et les croyances d'autres agents à partir du comportement observé sans communication explicite. Cette approche autoréflexive permet aux agents de coordonner de manière adaptative les tâches multi-agents complexes avec des performances supérieures.

de.title: MetaMind: Allgemeine und Kognitive Weltmodelle in Multi-Agent-Systemen durch Meta-Theorie des Geistes
de.summary: MetaMind führt ein Meta-Theorie-des-Geistes-Rahmenwerk ein, das KI-Agenten ermöglicht, Ziele und Überzeugungen anderer Agenten aus beobachtetem Verhalten vorherzusagen, ohne explizite Kommunikation. Dieser selbstreflexive Ansatz ermöglicht es Agenten, komplexe Multi-Agent-Aufgaben adaptiv zu koordinieren und eine überlegene Leistung zu erreichen.

es.title: MetaMind: Modelos del Mundo Generales y Cognitivos en Sistemas Multi-Agente mediante Metateoría de la Mente
es.summary: MetaMind introduce un marco de metateoría de la mente que permite a los agentes de IA predecir los objetivos y creencias de otros agentes a partir del comportamiento observado sin comunicación explícita. Este enfoque autorreflexivo permite a los agentes coordinar adaptativamente tareas complejas multi-agente con desempeño superior.

title: MC-Search: Evaluating and Enhancing Multimodal Agentic Search with Structured Long Reasoning Chains
summary: MC-Search is the first benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models in complex retrieval-augmented generation tasks requiring cross-modal reasoning across multiple steps. The work reveals systematic planning issues and introduces Search-Align, a framework for improving reasoning quality.
content:
arXiv:2603.00873v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: With the increasing demand for step-wise, cross-modal, and knowledge-grounded reasoning, multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are evolving beyond the traditional fixed retrieve-then-generate paradigm toward more sophisticated agentic multimodal retrieval-augmented generation (MM-RAG). Existing benchmarks, however, mainly focus on simplified QA with short retrieval chains, leaving adaptive planning and multimodal reasoning underexplored. We present MC-Search, the first benchmark for agentic MM-RAG with long, step-wise annotated reasoning chains spanning five representative reasoning structures. Each example specifies sub-questions, retrieval modalities, supporting facts, and intermediate answers, with fidelity ensured by HAVE (Hop-wise Attribution and Verification of Evidence), resulting in 3,333 high-quality examples averaging 3.7 hops. Beyond answer accuracy, MC-Search introduces new process-level metrics for reasoning quality, stepwise retrieval and planning accuracy. By developing a unified agentic MM-RAG pipeline, we benchmark six leading MLLMs and reveal systematic issues such as over- and under-retrieval and modality-misaligned planning. Finally, we introduce Search-Align, a process-supervised fine-tuning framework leveraging verified reasoning chains, showing that our data not only enables faithful evaluation but also improves planning and retrieval fidelity in open-source MLLMs.

zh.title: MC-Search：通过结构化长推理链评估和增强多模态代理搜索
zh.summary: MC-Search是首个评估多模态大型语言模型在需要跨模态推理的复杂检索增强生成任务中的基准。该工作揭示了系统规划问题，并引入了Search-Align框架以改进推理质量。

fr.title: MC-Search : Évaluation et amélioration de la recherche d'agents multimodaux avec des chaînes de raisonnement long structurées
fr.summary: MC-Search est le premier benchmark pour évaluer les grands modèles de langage multimodaux dans les tâches complexes de génération augmentée par récupération nécessitant un raisonnement multimodal sur plusieurs étapes. Le travail révèle des problèmes de planification systématique et introduit Search-Align, un cadre pour améliorer la qualité du raisonnement.

de.title: MC-Search: Bewertung und Verbesserung der multimodalen agentengestützten Suche mit strukturierten langen Reasoning-Ketten
de.summary: MC-Search ist die erste Benchmark zur Bewertung multimodaler großer Sprachmodelle in komplexen Aufgaben der Retrieval-Augmented Generation, die schrittübergreifendes modales Reasoning erfordern. Die Arbeit enthüllt systematische Planungsprobleme und stellt Search-Align vor, ein Framework zur Verbesserung der Reasoning-Qualität.

es.title: MC-Search: Evaluación y mejora de la búsqueda de agentes multimodales con cadenas de razonamiento largas estructuradas
es.summary: MC-Search es el primer benchmark para evaluar modelos de lenguaje grandes multimodales en tareas complejas de generación aumentada por recuperación que requieren razonamiento multimodal en múltiples pasos. El trabajo revela problemas de planificación sistemática e introduce Search-Align, un marco para mejorar la calidad del razonamiento.

title: BioProAgent: Neuro-Symbolic Grounding for Constrained Scientific Planning
summary: BioProAgent combines neural networks with symbolic reasoning to enable safe lab automation, achieving 95.6% hardware compliance compared to 21% for existing approaches. This neuro-symbolic framework is essential for deploying AI in irreversible physical environments where errors are costly.
content:
arXiv:2603.00876v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant reasoning capabilities in scientific discovery but struggle to bridge the gap to physical execution in wet-labs. In these irreversible environments, probabilistic hallucinations are not merely incorrect, but also cause equipment damage or experimental failure. To address this, we propose \textbf{BioProAgent}, a neuro-symbolic framework that anchors probabilistic planning in a deterministic Finite State Machine (FSM). We introduce a State-Augmented Planning mechanism that enforces a rigorous \textit{Design-Verify-Rectify} workflow, ensuring hardware compliance before execution. Furthermore, we address the context bottleneck inherent in complex device schemas by \textit{Semantic Symbol Grounding}, reducing token consumption by $\sim$6$\times$ through symbolic abstraction. In the extended BioProBench benchmark, BioProAgent achieves 95.6\% physical compliance (compared to 21.0\% for ReAct), demonstrating that neuro-symbolic constraints are essential for reliable autonomy in irreversible physical environments. \footnote{Code at https://github.com/YuyangSunshine/bioproagent and project at https://yuyangsunshine.github.io/BioPro-Project/}

zh.title: BioProAgent：约束科学规划的神经符号接地
zh.summary: BioProAgent结合神经网络和符号推理，实现安全的实验室自动化，硬件合规性达到95.6%，相比现有方法的21%有显著提升。这个神经符号框架对于在不可逆的物理环境中部署AI至关重要，其中错误的成本很高。

fr.title: BioProAgent : Ancrage neuro-symbolique pour la planification scientifique contrainte
fr.summary: BioProAgent combine les réseaux de neurones avec le raisonnement symbolique pour permettre l'automatisation sûre des laboratoires, atteignant une conformité matérielle de 95,6% par rapport à 21% pour les approches existantes. Ce cadre neuro-symbolique est essentiel pour déployer l'IA dans des environnements physiques irréversibles où les erreurs sont coûteuses.

de.title: BioProAgent: Neuro-symbolische Begründung für eingeschränkte wissenschaftliche Planung
de.summary: BioProAgent kombiniert neuronale Netze mit symbolischem Reasoning, um sichere Laborautomation zu ermöglichen und erreicht 95,6% Hardware-Konformität im Vergleich zu 21% bei bestehenden Ansätzen. Dieses neuro-symbolische Framework ist essentiell für den Einsatz von KI in irreversiblen physischen Umgebungen, wo Fehler kostspielig sind.

es.title: BioProAgent: Fundamentación neuro-simbólica para planificación científica restringida
es.summary: BioProAgent combina redes neuronales con razonamiento simbólico para permitir la automatización segura de laboratorios, logrando una conformidad de hardware del 95,6% en comparación con el 21% de los enfoques existentes. Este marco neuro-simbólico es esencial para desplegar IA en entornos físicos irreversibles donde los errores son costosos.

title: HiMAC: Hierarchical Macro-Micro Learning for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
summary: HiMAC introduces a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that separates high-level planning from low-level execution for long-horizon AI tasks. This structured approach achieves state-of-the-art performance with improved sample efficiency across text-based and visually grounded environments.
content:
arXiv:2603.00977v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents have recently demonstrated strong capabilities in interactive decision-making, yet they remain fundamentally limited in long-horizon tasks that require structured planning and reliable execution. Existing approaches predominantly rely on flat autoregressive policies, where high-level reasoning and low-level actions are generated within a single token sequence, leading to inefficient exploration and severe error propagation over extended trajectories. In this work, we propose HiMAC, a hierarchical agentic RL framework that explicitly decomposes long-horizon decision-making into macro-level planning and micro-level execution. HiMAC models reasoning as a structured blueprint generation process followed by goal-conditioned action execution, enabling robust long-horizon planning within LLM-based agents. To train this hierarchy efficiently, we introduce a critic-free hierarchical policy optimization paradigm that extends group-based reinforcement learning to bi-level structures through hierarchical relative advantage estimation. Furthermore, we propose an iterative co-evolution training strategy that alternates between planner exploration and executor adaptation, mitigating the non-stationarity inherent in hierarchical learning. Extensive experiments on ALFWorld, WebShop, and Sokoban demonstrate that HiMAC consistently outperforms strong prompting and reinforcement learning baselines, achieving state-of-the-art performance and substantially improved sample efficiency across both text-based and visually grounded environments. Our results show that introducing structured hierarchy, rather than increasing model scale alone, is a key factor for enabling robust long-horizon agentic intelligence.

zh.title: HiMAC：长视野LLM代理的分层宏观-微观学习
zh.summary: HiMAC引入了一个分层强化学习框架，将高层规划与低层执行分开，用于长视野AI任务。这种结构化方法在文本和视觉环境中都实现了最先进的性能，提高了样本效率。

fr.title: HiMAC : Apprentissage hiérarchique macro-micro pour les agents LLM de long horizon
fr.summary: HiMAC introduit un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement hiérarchique qui sépare la planification de haut niveau de l'exécution de bas niveau pour les tâches d'IA de long horizon. Cette approche structurée atteint les meilleures performances avec une efficacité d'échantillonnage améliorée dans les environnements basés sur le texte et visuellement ancrés.

de.title: HiMAC: Hierarchisches Makro-Mikro-Lernen für LLM-Agenten mit langem Horizont
de.summary: HiMAC führt ein hierarchisches Reinforcement-Learning-Framework ein, das Hochplanung von Ausführung auf niedriger Ebene für langfristige KI-Aufgaben trennt. Dieser strukturierte Ansatz erreicht neueste Leistung mit verbesserter Stichprobeneffizienz in Text- und visuell verankerten Umgebungen.

es.title: HiMAC: Aprendizaje jerárquico macro-micro para agentes LLM de largo horizonte
es.summary: HiMAC introduce un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo jerárquico que separa la planificación de alto nivel de la ejecución de bajo nivel para tareas de IA de largo horizonte. Este enfoque estructurado logra un rendimiento de última generación con eficiencia de muestra mejorada en entornos basados en texto y visualmente fundamentados.

title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
summary: This paper proposes using Scala 3's type system and capability tracking to enforce safety constraints on AI agents, preventing information leakage and malicious side effects. The approach allows agents to generate provably safe code without significant performance loss.
content:
arXiv:2603.00991v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents that interact with the real world through tool calls pose fundamental safety challenges: agents might leak private information, cause unintended side effects, or be manipulated through prompt injection. To address these challenges, we propose to put the agent in a programming-language-based "safety harness": instead of calling tools directly, agents express their intentions as code in a capability-safe language: Scala 3 with capture checking. Capabilities are program variables that regulate access to effects and resources of interest. Scala's type system tracks capabilities statically, providing fine-grained control over what an agent can do. In particular, it enables local purity, the ability to enforce that sub-computations are side-effect-free, preventing information leakage when agents process classified data. We demonstrate that extensible agent safety harnesses can be built by leveraging a strong type system with tracked capabilities. Our experiments show that agents can generate capability-safe code with no significant loss in task performance, while the type system reliably prevents unsafe behaviors such as information leakage and malicious side effects.

zh.title: 追踪能力以实现更安全的代理
zh.summary: 本文提出使用Scala 3的类型系统和能力追踪来对AI代理施加安全约束，防止信息泄露和恶意副作用。该方法允许代理生成可证明安全的代码，而不会显著降低性能。

fr.title: Capacités de suivi pour des agents plus sûrs
fr.summary: Cet article propose d'utiliser le système de types de Scala 3 et le suivi des capacités pour imposer des contraintes de sécurité aux agents d'IA, prévenant les fuites d'informations et les effets secondaires malveillants. L'approche permet aux agents de générer du code provablement sûr sans perte de performance significative.

de.title: Verfolgung von Fähigkeiten für sicherere Agenten
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt vor, das Typ-System von Scala 3 und die Fähigkeitsverfolgung zu verwenden, um Sicherheitsbeschränkungen für KI-Agenten durchzusetzen und so Informationslecks und bösartige Nebenwirkungen zu verhindern. Der Ansatz ermöglicht es Agenten, nachweislich sicheren Code ohne erhebliche Leistungseinbußen zu generieren.

es.title: Capacidades de seguimiento para agentes más seguros
es.summary: Este artículo propone utilizar el sistema de tipos de Scala 3 y el seguimiento de capacidades para imponer restricciones de seguridad en agentes de IA, previniendo fugas de información y efectos secundarios maliciosos. El enfoque permite que los agentes generen código demostrablemente seguro sin una pérdida significativa de rendimiento.

title: CollabEval: Enhancing LLM-as-a-Judge via Multi-Agent Collaboration
summary: CollabEval introduces a multi-agent evaluation framework where multiple models collaborate to assess AI-generated content, reducing bias and inconsistency compared to single-model evaluation. The approach demonstrates robust performance while maintaining efficiency through strategic consensus checking.
content:
arXiv:2603.00993v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized AI-generated content evaluation, with the LLM-as-a-Judge paradigm becoming increasingly popular. However, current single-LLM evaluation approaches face significant challenges, including inconsistent judgments and inherent biases from pre-training data. To address these limitations, we propose CollabEval, a novel multi-agent evaluation framework that implements a three-phase Collaborative Evaluation process: initial evaluation, multi-round discussion, and final judgment. Unlike existing approaches that rely on competitive debate or single-model evaluation, CollabEval emphasizes collaboration among multiple agents with strategic consensus checking for efficiency. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that CollabEval consistently outperforms single-LLM approaches across multiple dimensions while maintaining robust performance even when individual models struggle. The framework provides comprehensive support for various evaluation criteria while ensuring efficiency through its collaborative design.

zh.title: CollabEval：通过多智能体协作增强LLM-as-a-Judge
zh.summary: CollabEval引入了一个多智能体评估框架，其中多个模型协作评估AI生成的内容，相比单模型评估减少了偏差和不一致。该方法通过战略性的共识检查在保持效率的同时展现了强大的性能。

fr.title: CollabEval : Améliorer LLM-as-a-Judge par la collaboration multi-agents
fr.summary: CollabEval introduit un cadre d'évaluation multi-agents où plusieurs modèles collaborent pour évaluer le contenu généré par l'IA, réduisant les biais et les incohérences par rapport à l'évaluation par un seul modèle. L'approche démontre des performances robustes tout en maintenant l'efficacité grâce à la vérification stratégique du consensus.

de.title: CollabEval: Verbesserung von LLM-as-a-Judge durch Multi-Agent-Zusammenarbeit
de.summary: CollabEval führt ein Multi-Agent-Bewertungs-Framework ein, in dem mehrere Modelle zusammenarbeiten, um KI-generierte Inhalte zu bewerten, wobei Verzerrungen und Inkonsistenzen im Vergleich zu einer Einzelmodell-Bewertung reduziert werden. Der Ansatz zeigt robuste Leistung und Effizienz durch strategische Konsenssprüfung.

es.title: CollabEval: Mejorando LLM-as-a-Judge mediante colaboración multi-agente
es.summary: CollabEval introduce un marco de evaluación multi-agente donde múltiples modelos colaboran para evaluar contenido generado por IA, reduciendo sesgos e inconsistencias en comparación con la evaluación de un solo modelo. El enfoque demuestra un rendimiento robusto mientras mantiene la eficiencia mediante verificación de consenso estratégico.

title: MMCOMET: A Large-Scale Multimodal Commonsense Knowledge Graph for Contextual Reasoning
summary: MMCOMET is the first multimodal commonsense knowledge graph integrating text with visual information, containing over 900K multimodal triples. This resource enables richer, more contextually grounded reasoning for tasks like image captioning and storytelling.
content:
arXiv:2603.01055v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present MMCOMET, the first multimodal commonsense knowledge graph (MMKG) that integrates physical, social, and eventive knowledge. MMCOMET extends the ATOMIC2020 knowledge graph to include a visual dimension, through an efficient image retrieval process, resulting in over 900K multimodal triples. This new resource addresses a major limitation of existing MMKGs in supporting complex reasoning tasks like image captioning and storytelling. Through a standard visual storytelling experiment, we show that our holistic approach enables the generation of richer, coherent, and contextually grounded stories than those produced using text-only knowledge. This resource establishes a new foundation for multimodal commonsense reasoning and narrative generation.

zh.title: MMCOMET：用于上下文推理的大规模多模态常识知识图
zh.summary: MMCOMET是第一个集成文本和视觉信息的多模态常识知识图，包含超过900K多模态三元组。该资源为图像标题和叙事等任务提供更丰富、更加上下文化的推理。

fr.title: MMCOMET : Un graphique de connaissances de bon sens multimodal à grande échelle pour le raisonnement contextuel
fr.summary: MMCOMET est le premier graphique de connaissances de bon sens multimodal intégrant du texte et des informations visuelles, contenant plus de 900 000 triples multimodaux. Cette ressource permet un raisonnement plus riche et plus ancré dans le contexte pour des tâches comme la génération de légendes d'images et la narration.

de.title: MMCOMET: Ein großflächiges multimodales Common-Sense-Wissensgraph für kontextuelle Schlussfolgerung
de.summary: MMCOMET ist das erste multimodale Common-Sense-Wissensgraph, das Text mit visuellen Informationen integriert und über 900.000 multimodale Tripel enthält. Diese Ressource ermöglicht reichhaltigere, stärker kontextgebundene Schlussfolgerungen für Aufgaben wie Bildunterschriften und Geschichtenerzählen.

es.title: MMCOMET: Un gráfico de conocimientos de sentido común multimodal a gran escala para razonamiento contextual
es.summary: MMCOMET es el primer gráfico de conocimientos de sentido común multimodal que integra texto con información visual, conteniendo más de 900K triples multimodales. Este recurso permite un razonamiento más rico y contextualmente fundamentado para tareas como generación de descripciones de imágenes e historias.
